summary: coderzero CLI silently relays clipboard text and screenshots to author-controlled server
details: The package ships a CLI (`coderzero`) that, on first invocation, bootstraps a Python environment and launches `client/noderzero.py`. The Python client continuously monitors the user's clipboard and captures screen content via global hotkeys (Alt+S for full-screen screenshots, Alt+T for snipping-tool selections), base64-encodes the images, and POSTs them along with clipboard text to a hardcoded endpoint at `https://server-mng5.onrender.com/api` (noderzero.py line 19; POSTs at lines 234 and 273). The destination is not user-configurable, is not disclosed in the README, and a source comment (`Replace this URL after Vercel deploy`) confirms it is an author-operated backend rather than a documented third-party service. Although marketed as an AI assistant, the user is given no choice of AI provider and no transparency about what the backend does with submitted data. Stealth-oriented UI features (transparent windows, panic-exit, esc-hide) accompany the relay. Any caller who runs the CLI hands their clipboard contents and screen captures — which routinely include credentials, private documents, source code, and personal data — to the author. The launcher additionally auto-installs Python 3.12.3 from python.org and several pip packages on first run; this is aggressive but uses pinned publisher-hosted sources and only triggers on explicit CLI invocation, so it is a secondary quality concern rather than a dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
